card.title: mazer: mobile shell, portrait lock, and device harness
card.summary: Prove the phone-shell path for safe-area placement, exact touch registration, continuous stick intent, portrait behavior, and real-device confidence.
card.objective: Keep the canvas and every custom input surface in one live coordinate space while preserving a smooth 360-degree steering signal for grid-legal movement.
acceptanceCriteria: The rendered touch target and accepted game input agree across iPhone visual-viewport and safe-area origin changes.
Stick input publishes a continuous normalized vector without angular sectors or hysteresis buckets.
The grid resolver turns toward the strongest legal thumb projection and preserves bounded one-tile Smart Steering.
Physical phone feel, browser-chrome, and installed-PWA evidence remain required before terminal completion.
discoveries: The prior controller quantized the stick before the movement resolver, then discarded secondary-axis intent again, creating hard turn boundaries.
Same-size visual-viewport origin changes bypassed Phaser scale refresh and left cached hit-test bounds stale.
nextActions: Review and merge Mazer PR #78 after code review.
After a separately authorized production deployment, validate continuous-stick turns and touch alignment on the operator's iPhone.
Continue installed-PWA, browser-chrome, and authenticated keyboard proof without treating desktop emulation as physical-device closure.
blockers: Physical-device post-deploy feel and safe-area proof remain unavailable before merge and deployment.
evidence: Pure fixtures cover continuous vector ranking, direct upward T turns, legal corners, one-tile lane shifts, release cleanup, and live client-to-canvas coordinate mapping.
An exact upper-stick action at 390x844 moved the player from (26,36) to (26,35) in one accepted turn and released to idle with zero active controls.

entry.headline: Continuous stick and mobile touch-coordinate repair is ready for review
completed: Replaced stick segments and secondary-axis thresholding with one continuous normalized vector.
Ranked legal cardinal exits from the live thumb projection on every movement cadence.
Preserved direct turns, legal corners, bounded one-tile Smart Steering, and clean release semantics.
Aligned custom touch gestures and Phaser hit-testing with the live canvas rectangle.
discovered: The hard-to-turn behavior came from two quantization layers rather than the physical stick radius.
next: Merge PR #78 after review, then obtain separately authorized production and physical-iPhone feel proof.
blockers: Physical-device post-deploy feel proof remains open.
